title: Gastritis - NHS
text:
cy or fatty
do not drink alcohol
do not smoke
A pharmacist can help with mild indigestion A pharmacist can recommend:
medicines to help stop stomach acid from irritating your stomach and oesophagus, such as antacids and alginates medicines that reduce the amount of acid your stomach makes, such as proton pump inhibitors Some indigestion medicines are taken after eating, and some are taken before eating. Check the information leaflet that comes with the medicine.
Find a pharmacy
